expectations.text: recommendations.md discusses naming with explicit reference to grep-ability, retrieval, or localization (not just 'use good names')
expectations.evidence: Section 4 says 'Name things so that a grep for the feature name finds everything. If a feature is called "checkout", every file related to it should contain that string.'

expectations.text: recommendations.md recommends organizing code by feature / vertical slice rather than by horizontal layer
expectations.evidence: Section 5 'Collocate code, tests, types, and schema' recommends putting foo.ts/foo.test.ts/foo.types.ts/foo.schema.ts in the same directory rather than separate /tests, /types, /schemas trees.

expectations.text: recommendations.md warns against metaprogramming, reflection, or dynamic dispatch and explains why it hurts agents specifically
expectations.evidence: Section 4 says 'Avoid decorators / metaclasses / monkey-patching for core behavior' and 'Avoid magic strings that map to handlers' because agents follow imports and reimplement code they can't grep.

expectations.text: recommendations.md recommends typed public boundaries / type annotations at module seams
expectations.evidence: Section 1 'Make the type system the contract' prescribes strict TS, mypy --strict, and 'Validate all external inputs at the boundary with Zod / Pydantic / Valibot so that internal code has real types'.

expectations.text: recommendations.md recommends verification affordances (colocated tests, single command for lint+test+typecheck, fast feedback) and ties this to preventing agent hallucination spirals
expectations.evidence: Section 2 'Give the agent a fast, deterministic feedback loop' prescribes 'one command that does type-check + lint + unit tests in under ~30 seconds' and notes agents 'will confidently invent APIs that "look right" when they cannot verify', turning hallucination into a compile failure.

expectations.text: recommendations.md addresses file size, near-duplicates, or apply-model / str_replace breakage
expectations.evidence: Section 6 addresses file size ('A 2000-line file blows the context window') but does not mention near-duplicate blocks breaking str_replace / SEARCH-REPLACE / apply-model edits specifically.

expectations.text: recommendations.md grounds the reasoning in specific agent failure modes (e.g. mid-context position bias, grep misses, str_replace ambiguity, hallucinated APIs, spiral edits) rather than generic 'clean code' platitudes
expectations.evidence: Names concrete failure modes such as 'agents confidently invent APIs', 'any propagates silently', 'agents follow imports... if they can't find a symbol via grep... they assume it doesn't exist and reimplement it', and 'context window blows' on 2000-line files.

expectations.text: recommendations.md presents the list in a prioritized order (top 3, ranked list, or similar) rather than flat unordered bullets
expectations.evidence: Structured as 'Tier 1 — Highest leverage', 'Tier 2 — High leverage', 'Tier 3 — Meaningful polish' with numbered items 1-20 and a final 'minimal checklist you can apply this week'.
